channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: You may be eligible for lawsuit payout if you ate Breyers vanilla ice cream
item.description: A court ruling on the settlement has not yet occurred, with a final approval hearing set for Nov. 21.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, Fox 5

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: Salmon swim freely in the Klamath River for 1st time in a century after dams removed
item.description: HORNBROOK, Calif. (AP) — For the first time in more than a century, salmon are swimming freely along the Klamath River and its tributaries — a major watershed near the California-Oregon border — just days after the largest dam removal project in U.S. history was completed.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Demonstrations around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Family of Impact Plastics employee remember final phone conversations
item.description: "She was the healer of our family. She loved to cook for her family, for her grandchildren," Guillermo told NewsNation affiliate WJHL.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Simpson, NewsNation

item.title: Kenya’s deputy president defends himself before impeachment
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — Kenya’s deputy president, facing an impeachment motion in which he's accused of supporting anti-government protests in June and of being involved in corruption and other irregularities, has defended himself and confirmed that he would appear before parliament on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it," Michael Cohen said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Live Wobble Tracker: Map shows real-time movements of Hurricane Milton
item.description: Hurricane Milton, which reached Category 5 strength Monday, is heading right for Florida's west coast, with a potential landfall near or at Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KRON4

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court won’t hear Pennsylvania legislators' challenge to executive actions expanding voting access
item.description: The Supreme Court decided Monday it won’t take up a case to restore a lawsuit by Pennsylvania Republicans that challenged executive actions expanding voting access on the basis that only state legislatures can regulate federal elections.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Florida doctor arrested, accused of running deadly 'pill mill'
item.description: The investigation started after several pharmacists complained about the large amounts of oxycodone being prescribed.
item.dc:creator: Hannah O'Gara, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court won’t take up challenge to Jack Smith’s Trump Twitter data access
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday declined to take up a challenge by social platform X, formerly known as Twitter, to court rulings that forced the platform to turn over data on former President Trump’s account to special counsel Jack Smith.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Park ranger dies responding to call for help when rescue boat capsizes on lake
item.description: A park ranger died Sunday at Voyageurs National Park in northern Minnesota after his rescue vessel capsized while helping three civilians stranded in rough weather.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Turning Point wants to revolutionize how Republicans turn out voters. Some are skeptical
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Turning Point’s representatives have made two things clear in meetings with state and local Republican leaders — Donald Trump has blessed their conservative organization to help lead his get-out-the-vote effort, and local party officials ought to use the group's new voter mobilizat
item.dc:creator: DAN MERICA and BRIAN SLODYSKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina first responders exchange vows among chaos in Hurricane Helene aftermath
item.description: A North Carolina first responder couple honored their love for each other with a commitment ceremony on Oct. 2 after Hurricane Helene robbed them of their initial wedding plans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hurricane Milton is growing stronger as it blows toward Florida's Tampa Bay region
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Hurricane Milton quickly intensified Sunday and is on track to become a major hurricane with the Tampa Bay area in its sights, putting Florida on edge and triggering evacuation orders along a coast still reeling from Helene’s devastation.
item.dc:creator: FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending criminal probe of US woman's death
item.description: Swiss police arrested several people following the death of an unidentified 64-year-old woman from the U.S. Midwest who on Sept. 23 became the first person to use the device.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: US disaster relief chief blasts false claims about Helene response as a 'truly dangerous narrative'
item.description: The U.S. government’s top disaster relief official says false claims and conspiracy theories about the federal response to Hurricane Helene, which are being spread most prominently by Donald Trump, are “demoralizing” aid workers and creating fear in people who need recovery assistance.
item.dc:creator: Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Helene flooding in NC stirs yellow jacket swarms, prompting distribution of Benadryl and EpiPens
item.description: Swarms of stinging insects in North Carolina are swarming those trying to recover from the aftermath of Hurricane Helene, prompting the distribution of Benadryl and epinephrine injections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what Spirit Halloween seeks in an 'ideal' retail location
item.description: Spirit Halloween made headlines earlier this week after firing back at "SNL" over a sketch that mocked the company's choice of retail locations.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Couple reunited after volunteer rescue pilot threatened with arrest in North Carolina
item.description: Jordan Seidhom, who decided to go help those impacted by Helene with his own helicopter on his own dime, was able to rescue Susan Coffey off a mountainside. But, before he could go back for her husband, Mike, and his own son and co-pilot, he was threatened with arrest.
item.dc:creator: Jody Barr, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton growing stronger as it approaches Florida: NHC
item.description: Milton has strengthed into a hurricane in the Gulf of Mexico, according to the National Hurricane Center.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KRON4

item.title: See the 10 most – and least – valuable college majors: study
item.description: A new study from financial publisher Bankrate found that STEM college majors dominate the ranking of valuable fields of study – but money isn't the only consideration.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Verdugo's go-ahead single and acrobatic catch lift Yankees over Royals 6-5 in ALDS opener
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Alex Verdugo hit a tiebreaking single in the seventh inning and saved at least one run with a sliding catch along the left-field line, boosting the New York Yankees over the Kansas City Royals 6-5 on Saturday night in their AL Division Series opener.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KTLA 5

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: Few things are as universally reviled as a loud phone conversation infringing on your personal space. (Mosquitoes and taxes, aside.) Doing it on loudspeaker is even more incendiary.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Does a cumulus cloud really weigh a million pounds?
item.description: Just because something floats doesn't mean it is weightless. A great example is when oil is poured onto water. The oil will float on top of the water, but that doesn't mean it has no weight.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Bacca, KTLA 5

item.title: After the deluge, the lies: Misinformation and hoaxes about Helene cloud the recovery
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The facts emerging from Hurricane Helene's destruction are heartrending: Businesses and homes destroyed, whole communities nearly wiped out, hundreds of lives lost, hundreds of people missing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Inside the North Carolina mountain town that Hurricane Helene nearly wiped off the map
item.description: CHIMNEY ROCK VILLAGE, N.C. (AP) — The stone tower that gave this place its name was nearly a half billion years in the making — heated and thrust upward from deep in the Earth, then carved and eroded by wind and water.
item.dc:creator: ALLEN G. BREED, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 Florida cities home to the worst drivers in the U.S., study says
item.description: A Tennessee city has once again been given the dubious title of home to the worst drivers in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Need a passport? You can now get one faster
item.description: U.S. passport processing time has been reduced by two weeks at most, the State Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Shock of deadly floods is a reminder of Appalachia’s risk from violent storms in a warming climate
item.description: HENDERSONVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Hurricane Helene dumped trillions of gallons of water hundreds of miles inland, devastating communities nestled in mountains far from the threat of storm surge or sea level rise.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4
